title: config: wrong entry for runtime[s]
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**
/kind bug

**Description**
When specifying `--runtime` on a blank system (with no existing config files), there wrong value gets written to the config file.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Install _podman_ and all other tools and libs as user under `/home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/` on a brand new VM.

2. Execute
```
/home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/podman --log-level=debug --tmpdir /tmp/user/1000/libpod/tmp --conmon /home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/conmon --network-cmd-path /home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/slirp4netns --runtime /home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/runc --storage-driver overlay --storage-opt "overlay.mount_program=/home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/fuse-overlayfs" run hello-world 
```

3. Check `/home/tobwen/.config/containers/libpod.conf`

**Describe the results you received:**
(warning: this has been trimmed down for reasons)
```
runtime = "/home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/runc"

[runtimes]
  runc = ["/usr/bin/runc", "/usr/sbin/runc", "/usr/local/bin/runc", "/usr/local/sbin/runc", "/sbin/runc", "/bin/runc", "/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc", "/run/current-system/sw/bin/runc"]
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
runtime = "runc"

[runtimes]
runc = [ "/home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/runc" ]
```

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:            1.6.3-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.5
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
```

tobwen@pgsql:~/podman/usr/local/bin$ ./podman --tmpdir /tmp/user/1000/libpod/tmp info --debug

  compiler: gc

  BuildahVersion: 1.11.3

    package: Unknown
    path: /home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.3-dev, commit: bc758d8bd98a29ac3aa4f62a886575bfec0e39a1'
  Distribution:
    distribution: debian

  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0

      runc version 1.0.0-rc9+dev
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
n/a (built manually)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Debian 9 (64-bit) on VirtualBox 6 on Windows 10 on AMD 64

comments.body:
it looks like the expected behaviour.

You have specified `--runtime /home/tobwen/podman/usr/local/bin/runc` so that setting is written to the configuration file.

That is different than specifying `runc`, as when you specify an absolute path then exactly that executable will be tried.  If you specify the runtime by name, then Podman will try to find it at  the specified locations.

Then `--help` might be wrong. It tells me to specify a path, not a name.
```
--runtime string            Path to the OCI-compatible binary used to run containers, default is /usr/bin/runc
```

So when a path gets specified at `--runtime`, `[runtimes]` shouldn't be written at all, since you cannot find _runc_ or _crun_ in there.
@giuseppe I thought the name could be a full path, if so shouldn't we record the full path that the user specified.
Seems like it *can* be a full path. I just thought, runtimes path should go under _[runtimes]_ to be able to switch between _runc_ and _crun_ just by specifying a name.

Yes the runtimes are more for the distribution to sort of guess where the OCI runtimes are installed (In my opinion) But if the user tells you exactly where the OCI runtime is, I don't see the reason to have to search through for it.

`--runtime` also short-circuits the logic for the `[runtimes]` section of the config. If a path is given, it is assumed that you're overwriting the entire `[runtimes]` section with a path not present in it.

On these config merging issues: I would rather we give priority to https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4214 and focus on not requiring initial Podman setup to use an arcane set of flags to get the rootless config file in a good state.
